authoringAttempts.method: set bold of font object of text object of d to true (whole paragraph), then un-bold words 2 thru 2
authoringAttempts.result: Word RESTRUCTURED the runs instead of emitting a negation: 'BOLDED ' <w:b/>, 'UNBOLD ' no rPr, 'BOLDED' <w:b/>, paragraph-mark rPr <w:b/>. grep for 'w:b w:val="0"' -> 0 hits; 3x 'w:b/>'. NOT a true negation run — discarded.
authoringAttempts.honestNote: Direct character bolding does not produce negation runs in Word 16.77.1 — Word splits runs instead.

authoringAttempts.method: set bold of font object of (get Word style style normal of d) to true (Normal STYLE bold), then un-bold words 2 thru 2
authoringAttempts.result: TRUE negation run: styles.xml Normal has <w:rPr><w:b/></w:rPr>; document.xml UNBOLD run carries <w:b w:val="0"/> (grep: 1 hit), BOLDED runs have no run-level w:b (inherit style bold). This is the checked-in fixture.
authoringAttempts.pidSafety: initial=0 final=0 both attempts; closed by name; Word never quit

cloneImportFindings.marks_on_UNBOLD: bold:{value:false} (BOOLEAN false — the style-cascade import path) + textStyle (Calibri 12pt from Normal)
cloneImportFindings.marks_on_BOLDED: bold:{value:true} (style-inherited bold materialized as a mark)
cloneImportFindings.bugFoundAndFixed: toQueryState() in src/renderer/bridge/state-sync.ts only treated the STRING '0' as a negation attr (a.value !== '0'); the boolean false from the style-cascade import read as bold=ON. Fixed with markOn(val) mirroring the fork's renderDOM semantics (null -> ON; '0' or falsy -> OFF) for bold/italic/underline/strike.
cloneImportFindings.harnessHoleFoundAndFixed: the PM suite's t() counted string returns (all failure paths) as PASSES — the red negation test was reported green with detail 'UNBOLD reported bold'. t() now fails on string returns.

suiteTest: [1] imported negation run reports bold=false (converter attrs) — opens the fixture via window.__WC_FIXTURE_NEGATION() (gen-fixture inlined, hermetic), asserts getState().bold === false at UNBOLD and === true at style-bolded BOLDED. PM suite: 43/43.
overall: PASS after fixes — a real-Word negation run round-trips into truthful ribbon state; the oracle leg caught one genuine state-sync bug and one test-harness truthfulness hole.
